description: Port the mode detection logic (overlay/libexec/k3os/mode) into Go. Extend the existing internal/mode package with a Detect() function that reads /proc/cmdline for k3os.mode=, checks for K3OS_STATE partition via blkid, checks filesystem type, and implements the 30-second wait loop with fallback. This replaces the mode shell script.
steps: Write tests first in internal/mode/detect_test.go covering: cmdline k3os.mode=disk returns 'disk'; cmdline rescue returns 'shell'; blkid finds K3OS_STATE returns 'disk'; K3OS_MODE env var is respected; non-tmpfs root returns 'local'; fallback_mode from cmdline is used; timeout returns error when no mode found; mode file is written to /run/k3os/mode on success
Define interfaces for the external dependencies: CmdlineReader (reads /proc/cmdline), BlkidChecker (checks for partition labels), StatfsChecker (checks filesystem type), FileWriter (writes mode file)
Implement Detect() function in internal/mode/detect.go with the full mode detection logic matching the shell script behavior
Implement real OS implementations of the interfaces
Run golangci-lint run ./... and fix any issues
Run go test ./internal/mode/... and verify all tests pass
Commit with message 'feat(mode): port mode detection shell script to Go'
acceptance_criteria: Detect() handles all mode determination paths: cmdline override, rescue, blkid K3OS_STATE, env var, filesystem type check, fallback, timeout
Mode result is written to state file at /run/k3os/mode
Tests verify each detection path independently using mocked dependencies
